member.name: Gill Furniss
member.role: MP for Sheffield Brightside and Hillsborough

addresses: SOAR Works, 14 Knutton Road, Sheffield, S5 9NU

member.synopsis: Gill Furniss is the Labour MP for Sheffield Brightside and Hillsborough , and has been an MP continually since 5 May 2016.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Cardiovascular Diseases: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what his planned timetable is for publication of the cardiovascular disease Modern Service Framework.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Cardiovascular Disease Modern Service Framework (CVD MSF) is being finalised to ensure it reflects the strongest available evidence and is due to be published soon.The CVD MSF will support consistent, high quality, and equitable care whilst fostering ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Active Travel
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what information her Department holds on the proportion of short stages in towns and cities in England that are currently walked and cycled.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Estimates of the proportion of short stages in towns and cities in England that are walked and cycled are obtained from the National Travel Survey. In 2024, 48% of stages of less than 5 miles made by residents of towns and cities in England were walked or...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Active Travel: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of providing multi-year funding for Active Travel England grants.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Following publication of the Devolution White Paper in 2024, the majority of grants to local authorities are consolidated and are provided on a multi-year funding basis, including Active Travel England funding. This reduces burdens on authorities and allo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Mental Health Services: Menopause
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to support the mental health of women going through (a) menopause, (b) perimenopause and (c) hormonal treatment related to menopause.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the impact menopause can have on women’s lives.The Renewed Women’s Health Strategy identifies menopause as a core women’s health priority, recognising its impact on women’s health, wellbeing, work, and quality of life. The strate...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Mental Health Services: Menopause
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department has identified links between menopause and a heightened risk of suicide; and what support his Department provides to safeguard the mental health of women reaching menopause.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Menopause can have a significant impact on a woman’s mental health, and low mood and anxiety are recognised symptoms that some women may experience during the perimenopause and menopause.The Department recognises the importance of ensuring that women expe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Endometriosis: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the potential impact of single points of access for non-urgent gynaecology referrals, specialist ultrasound and community diagnostic routes on reducing di
writtenQuestions.answerText: From October 2026, all providers, supported by integrated care boards, are expected to ensure that all appropriate requests and referrals flow through a Single Point of Access (SPoA) model, to support patients to receive rapid specialist assessment and a ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Menopause: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that menopause training is included in routine primary care continuing professional development and the women’s health workforce model to ensure consis
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the ongoing need to ensure healthcare practitioners have sufficient knowledge of women’s health, including menopause, to provide the best possible care.General practitioners (GPs) are responsible for ensuring their own clinical k...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Menopause: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what plans his Department has to ensure that Integrated Care Boards have clear pathways for access to specialist menopause advice and escalation for complex cases, including premature ovarian insuf
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Renewed Women’s Health Strategy, published in April 2026, committed to redesigning clinical pathways, including for menopause.These redesigned pathways will create roadmaps for health systems to use and adapt for local needs that will enable women to ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Endometriosis: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department plans to include metrics on endometriosis diagnostic times, access to imaging, menopause prescribing, treatment review and referral variation in the women’s health data dashb
writtenQuestions.answerText: The women's health data dashboard is available on the NHS Futures website and is available to anyone working within the health and care sector who requires insight into women's health. The dashboard records need, access, outcomes, and experiences across a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Endometriosis: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to support Integrated Care Boards to implement NICE guideline NG73 on endometriosis in primary care, including training for GPs and practice nurses on recognisin
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government acknowledges the importance of ensuring healthcare professionals are adequately trained and educated on women’s health conditions, including endometriosis, and we have taken action to address this.NHS England encourages adherence to guidanc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Heart Valve Disease: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of treatment delays on outcomes for patients with heart valve disease.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We recognise the importance of reducing waiting times. We are making progress - hitting our target of 65% against the 18 week standard this March and delivering largest single month elective performance improvement in 17 years - excluding the pandemic. Ou...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what estimate she has made of the level of the rise in water connection charges for domestic consumers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Water connection charges for domestic consumers vary by water company and are set on a cost-recovery basis in line with Ofwat’s charging rules. Administration fees and application charges for new connections differ by water company. There is no centrally ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Neurofibromatosis
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, a) what steps are being taken to inform those living with Neurofibromatosis Type 1 (NF1) that they are entitled to earlier screening for breast cancer, and b) what steps are being taken to improve
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to improving cancer screening services in line with the National Cancer Plan and as part of the 10-Year Health Plan’s shift from treatment to prevention.Neurofibromatosis type 1 (NF1) affects individuals differently. An indivi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Somalia: Human Rights
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what recent assessment her Department has made of the human rights situation in Somalia.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We remain deeply concerned by the human rights situation in Somalia, including the devastating conflict-related violence against women and children, and deteriorating media freedom.The UK advocates for greater respect for human rights through targeted pro...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Pre-school Education: Sheffield
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the equity of the Early Years funding formula on families and childcare providers in Sheffield.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department uses the early years national funding formulae (EYNFF) to allocate early years entitlement funding to local authorities in a fair and transparent way.The formulae ensure each area receives a base rate for each age group individually, regard...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Council Tax: Arrears
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of the findings of the Money Advice Trust’s Lost in the system report; and if he will make a statement.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is grateful to the Money Advice Trust and the important work carried out by the National Debt line to support people facing financial difficulties.We acknowledge the concerns and issues raised in the report regarding council tax collection ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is grateful to the Money Advice Trust and the important work carried out by the National Debt line to support people facing financial difficulties.We acknowledge the concerns and issues raised in the report regarding council tax collection ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Yemen: Peace Negotiations
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps she is taking to support a peaceful political settlement in Yemen.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK remains committed to working towards lasting peace and prosperity for the Yemeni people. We continue to support the work of the UN Special Envoy for Yemen and use our role as penholder for Yemen at the UN Security Council to galvanise international...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Yemen: Humanitarian Aid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps she is taking to support humanitarian aid organisations operating in Yemen.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK remains committed to working towards lasting peace and prosperity for the Yemeni people. We continue to support the work of the UN Special Envoy for Yemen and use our role as penholder for Yemen at the UN Security Council to galvanise international...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs: Speech and Language Disorders
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department plans to take to improve a) universal, b) targeted and c) specialist speech, language and communication support.

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education
